data.command: echo '--- IDAHO-VAULT CONTEXT INJECTION ---' && echo 'Date:' $(date '+%Y-%m-%d') && echo 'Branch:' $(git branch --show-current) && echo '' && echo 'LEVELSET: Read !/!/LEVELSET-CURRENT.md for current vault state.' && echo 'CLAUDE.md: Loaded automatically — single source of truth for operations.' && echo 'DECISIONS: Governance file at !/DECISIONS.md — read only, do not use as relay surface.' && echo '' && echo 'Vault owner: Logan Finney — journalist, Idaho Reports / Idaho Public Television' && echo 'All committed content is ON THE RECORD and PUBLISHABLE.' && echo '--- END CONTEXT INJECTION ---'

content.text:
Fix the following issues. The issues can be from different files or can overlap on same lines in one file.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @!/!/SESSION-2026-03-29-artifacts/Linear - agent chat - Greeting IDAHO-VAULT.md around lines 733 - 753, The Linear workspace "Agent Guidance" duplicates canonical vault rules, creating dual-source drift; update the guidance text (the Agent Guidance block/example) to remove verbatim protocol content and instead reference vault canonical files (Constitution.md, PROTOCOL.md, !ADMIN/) with explicit directions like "See vault: !/CONSTITUTION.md" and keep only minimal operational bullets (authority, reporting, fallback steps), and add a short "sync protocol" note that instructs agents/maintainers to: (1) treat vault as source-of-truth, (2) update Linear only via a documented sync PR when vault changes, and (3) flag discrepancies to Logan—locate and modify the Agent Guidance content and the lines referencing advisory/canonical behavior (the existing advisory text around "Don't treat Linear as the vault" and "advisory") to point to the new reference-based guidance.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @!/!/SESSION-2026-03-29-artifacts/Linear - agent chat - Greeting.md around lines 1 - 1798, The new large documentation file (!/!/SESSION-2026-03-29-artifacts/Linear - agent chat - Greeting.md) was added without a LEVELSET/Governance check and its path/name likely violates VAULT conventions; before merging, implement an enforcement step so the CODE AUTHORITY agent performs a LEVELSET verification and LOGAN must sign off on the PR to main, validate and (if needed) move/rename the file to follow VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md and VAULT-ZONES.md patterns, and update the PR template or CI (e.g., a check named "require-levelset" or rule in the repo workflow) to block merges to main until the LEVELSET and Logan-review approvals are present.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @!/!/SESSION-2026-03-29-artifacts/Linear - agent chat - Select IDAHO-VAULT project.md at line 387, The TITLE assignment using single quotes around the GitHub Actions expression is vulnerable to shell injection; replace that pattern by reading the pull request title safely from the GitHub event JSON (use the GITHUB_EVENT_PATH file and parse with jq or another JSON-safe reader) and assign the resulting string to the TITLE variable (avoid embedding ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }} inside single-quoted shell strings); update the code that sets TITLE and any consuming logic to use the jq-derived value so quotes and special characters in the PR title are handled safely.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @.github/workflows/linear-brief.yml around lines 86 - 108, The webhook secret is being removed by scrub() before validation, so modify the logic to extract provided_secret from payload (e.g., payload.get("webhook_secret", "")) prior to calling scrub(payload); keep existing checks that compare provided_secret with configured_secret using hmac.compare_digest, and ensure scrub() is still applied to payload for later use; update any branches that set live_write (payload_live_write) to use the scrubbed or original payload as appropriate but do the secret extraction before scrub() so provided_secret isn't emptied.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @.obsidian/community-plugins.json around lines 9 - 14, The added plugin entries in .obsidian/community-plugins.json ("obsidian-local-rest-api", "templater-obsidian", "mcp-tools", "smart-connections") require a security review: inspect each plugin's repository/docs to list required permissions, network or code execution capabilities, and any data access (focus on obsidian-local-rest-api for exposed endpoints, templater-obsidian for script execution, and smart-connections for external API/AI calls); confirm whether enabling these changes to .obsidian/ needs LOGAN approval per vault governance and document that decision; specifically research "mcp-tools" to determine its purpose, requested permissions, and risk profile and report findings and a recommended allow/deny decision back in the PR.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @.obsidian/plugins/smart-connections/styles.css around lines 543 - 552, Remove the redundant duplicate margin declaration in the .sc-tool-calls-container CSS rule: keep the intended margin (pick either "0.5rem 0" or "10px") and delete the other so only a single margin property remains in the .sc-tool-calls-container rule to avoid one value being silently overridden.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @.obsidian/plugins/smart-connections/styles.css around lines 480 - 488, The .sc-context-container selector has duplicate margin declarations (one for vertical spacing and one for a uniform 10px) so remove the unwanted one to avoid shadowing; if you intend a uniform margin keep the later uniform value (remove the vertical-only margin declaration) on .sc-context-container, otherwise keep the vertical-only margin and remove the uniform 10px declaration.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @.obsidian/plugins/smart-connections/styles.css around lines 171 - 174, Remove the redundant font-size declaration in the CSS selector ".mod-right-split .sc-list .sc-result": the declaration "font-size: var(--font-text-size);" is immediately overridden by "font-size: 0.88rem;", so delete the first line and keep the intended "font-size: 0.88rem;" (or replace with the variable if you prefer the var value) to eliminate the duplicate.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @.obsidian/plugins/smart-connections/styles.css around lines 365 - 371, Remove the duplicate nested #settings rule inside .sc-chat-container: locate the second occurrence of ".sc-chat-container { #settings { ... } }" (the block shown) and delete that nested #settings block so only the original definition for #settings (previous block under .sc-chat-container) remains; ensure no other styles are accidentally removed and CSS structure/braces remain valid.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @.obsidian/plugins/templater-obsidian/main.js at line 16, The HTTP response check in getRequest incorrectly uses `t.status < 200 && t.status >= 300` (impossible) so non-2xx responses are treated as success; update the condition in the getRequest implementation to detect non-successful status with `t.status < 200 || t.status >= 300` and ensure the function handles that branch by rejecting/throwing or returning an error (preserve existing error handling behavior). Locate the getRequest function and replace the logical AND with a logical OR when checking `t.status`, keeping the same error flow (throw/new P or console.error) used elsewhere in the function.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @.obsidian/plugins/templater-obsidian/main.js at line 17, The code currently calls window.eval on user script text (in the ji / generate_user_script_functions / user_script_functions code path), which allows arbitrary vault code execution; replace this by NOT using eval: load user scripts via a safer loader such as dynamic module import from a Blob/URL (or execute in a dedicated sandbox like a sandboxed iframe or Worker) or use a vetted Function-wrapper that only exposes a strict, minimal API; ensure the loader still validates that the module default export (i.default or o.exports) is a function or object of functions and preserves the existing error handling (the P(...) errors) when loading/parsing fails, and remove any direct uses of window.eval in ji / generate_user_script_functions / user_script_functions.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @!/AGENTS.md at line 40, Add a new Tier 5 "Coordination (Support)" definition to the Capability Tiers section (referencing the Linear (Workspace Manager) agent) that enumerates allowed actions (manage Linear issues/labels/milestones, curate decision logs, provide coaching via Linear comments, surface context) and disallowed actions (no GitHub repo access, no vault commits/edits, no command execution, no direct governance/vault modifications), and add a "PASSIVE INPUT" definition to PROTOCOL.md describing read-only context/advice surfaced via external coordination platforms (e.g., Linear comments/issues visible to LOGAN/swarm) to clarify boundaries; ensure the change is authored by CODE AUTHORITY per governance modification rules before committing.

- Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @- GitHub Docs - Approving workflow runs from forks.md around lines 8 - 22, This document reproduces substantial GitHub Docs content; replace the copied sections (e.g., the "Approving workflow runs on a pull request from a public fork" paragraph and steps including the callout to inspect ".github/workflows/") with either a concise original-summary plus a direct link to the GitHub Docs source, or confirm and document that the GitHub Docs license permits full reproduction and add an explicit attribution line; also preserve and surface your vault governance note that changes to .github/workflows/ require CODE AUTHORITY review so reviewers see that policy before merging.

data.command: echo '--- IDAHO-VAULT CONTEXT INJECTION ---' && echo 'Date:' $(date '+%Y-%m-%d') && echo 'Branch:' $(git branch --show-current) && echo '' && echo 'LEVELSET: Read !/!/LEVELSET-CURRENT.md for current vault state.' && echo 'CLAUDE.md: Loaded automatically — single source of truth for operations.' && echo 'DECISIONS: Governance file at !/DECISIONS.md — read only, do not use as relay surface.' && echo '' && echo 'Vault owner: Logan Finney — journalist, Idaho Reports / Idaho Public Television' && echo 'All committed content is ON THE RECORD and PUBLISHABLE.' && echo '--- END CONTEXT INJECTION ---'
